description_long: This object is a vintage cathode-ray tube (CRT) television with a boxy, rectangular shape and a prominent convex screen. The front features a classic test pattern display, surrounded by a thick, dark bezel and a brown outer casing. On the right side of the front panel, there are two large rotary dials and a speaker grille, typical of mid-20th-century television sets. The sides and back of the television are solid, with the rear housing bulging outward to accommodate the CRT tube. The materials appear to be primarily plastic and glass, with some metal components likely used for the dials and internal structure. This television would be found in urban environments as discarded electronics, retro decor, or in secondhand shops.
description: A vintage CRT television with a convex screen, brown casing, rotary dials, and a test pattern display on the front.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex screen with a test pattern, two rotary dials, and a speaker grille on the right.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the television and the protruding CRT back, with a plain brown and gray panel.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the large, bulging CRT housing and the solid brown casing.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to the other side, showing the depth and plain panel without controls.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, dark bezel with a matte finish, rectangular shape, and no visible branding.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of two rotary dials and a speaker grille on the lower right front panel.
